what_it_does: A living status ledger for the 'company' (Vi composition suite) codebase, intended to let a fresh AI enter the repo, read the rules and map, then act correctly without re-deriving boundaries. It records what is built and proven, what is not built yet, how to run the system, the laws that bind agents, gotchas, and open work.
observations: Dated as of 2026-06-07, with later 2026-06-13/2026-06-14 Mirror-Registry and Session Fabric rollup sections appended.
Contains an auto-maintained list of 232 acceptance suites between SUITES markers.
Insists that tests/*.py are the convergence record and that claims must be proven by fresh use, not assertion.

Details many built subsystems: reactive engine, fabric/AI nodes, registries, bridge + MCP faces, RHM, governed self-modification, decision-to-implementation wire, F1 altitude/presentation learning, voice endpoints, operational console, agent-session exporter, session supervisor, Mirror-Registry System, render declarations, UI Contract corpus, session registry, cross-session channels/gatherings/edges, search-to-handle-to-act, point-in-time session launch.
Maintains an honest ledger taxonomy: proven-by-real-operation, built+service-verified with lead live-probe queued, and not-built-yet.
Lists unbuilt items explicitly: live hardware voice loop, twin predictive strength, embed-based retrieval, Tauri wrap, in-place arbitrary self-mod.
Includes run instructions, binding laws, and gotchas such as pkill exit 144 noise and extension syntax-gate cold-start cost.
standouts: The acceptance-suite list and drift_acceptance.py are treated as the machine-enforced convergence record.
The Mirror-Registry System is a large recent thread spanning contracts, introspection, registries, projection, cap:// address wiring, and supervisor spawn-flag posture refactor.
The explicit honest-ledger split between 'proven by real operation', 'built + service-verified, lead live-probe queued', and 'not built yet' avoids green-painting.
Self-modification is additive, git-reversible, governed, and backed by a three-stream audit ledger plus operator checkpoints.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with type, register, module, aliases, tags, status.
Uses internal wiki-style links like [[Company — read first]] and [[Company Map]].
Contains auto-maintained HTML comment markers for the acceptance-suite block.
Mixes high-level prose with deep technical detail, test names, and handoff file references.
Uses honest negation phrases: 'NOT yet', 'NOT built', 'NOT here yet'.
Repeats single-source / registry-is-truth / fail-loud principles throughout.
concerns: The file is extremely long and dense, so a fresh reader may struggle to locate the relevant boundary despite the stated purpose.
Factual blocks auto-regenerate but prose must be updated by integration, creating a dual-maintenance risk.
Multiple overlapping date headers and inline handoff references could drift if the handoff files move or change.
The huge acceptance-suite list is auto-maintained but may become visual noise if not folded or indexed.
Several 'open question' and 'flagged for operator' notes are embedded in prose rather than tracked as formal tasks.
notes: This is the operational heartbeat document: a self-updating status board plus prose changelog that tells an agent what is real, what is queued, and what is forbidden.
questions: Where are the referenced handoff files located and how are they versioned relative to this file?
What is the exact schema and inclusion rule for the auto-maintained SUITES block?
How is the 'living' status enforced beyond the drift_acceptance.py test?
What is the precise boundary between factual content that auto-updates and prose that must be edited by integration?
What determines whether a queued live-probe is assigned to the lead versus this worker?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be a self-contained current status a fresh AI can read and act on; the file largely does that, though its length and embedded technical depth mean a fresh reader still needs AGENTS.md and MAP.md first, which the file itself acknowledges.
apparent_themes: Prove by use / tests as the convergence record.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the codebase legible to autonomous agents and to stop them from treating unbuilt capabilities as done; it wants to be the ground-truth ledger that binds every capability to a test and every gap to an explicit 'not built yet' statement.
novelty: The unusual combination of a human-readable markdown status document with machine-enforced auto-regeneration, an acceptance-suite convergence record, and an explicit 'honest ledger' taxonomy (proven / queued / not-built) rather than a conventional changelog.
contribution: It contributes a maintained, auditable snapshot of build truth that a fused system can consult to avoid hallucinating capabilities; the acceptance-suite registry and the proven/queued/not-built taxonomy are reusable patterns for grounding agent behavior.
summary_for_embedding: STATE.md is the living status ledger for the company Vi composition suite, listing built and proven capabilities, acceptance suites, unbuilt work, run instructions, and binding laws.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to give any entering agent an honest, current, self-contained picture of what the system can do and what remains open, without re-deriving boundaries.
